page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-4GB is a budget-tier virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs across 2 physical cores with a single thread per core, paired with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates limited performance capabilities, reflecting its shared CPU allocation. The server scores in the bottom 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations. However, it achieves average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside an average Geekbench single-core score. Multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations show weaker results, placing in the bottom 10% to 25% across Passmark and Geekbench suites.

Due to the shared CPU model and lack of local storage, this server is not designed for resource-intensive workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-demand, non-critical applications such as basic web serving, lightweight background processes, or development and testing environments where continuous CPU performance is not required.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-4GB is an entry-level virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7542 processor operating at 2.0 GHz, providing 2 shared vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU resources. Performance benchmarks show that the server resides primarily in the lower tiers, with stress-ng CPU and large-block memory bandwidth results falling into the bottom 10%. It demonstrates moderate capability in single-threaded tasks, such as gzip compression and Geekbench single-core tests, where it achieves average marks. Given its shared CPU allocation and lack of bundled storage, this server is best suited for low-intensity workloads, including lightweight web serving, basic utility scripts, and development environments where resource demands are minimal and cost efficiency is prioritized over sustained high performance.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-2xCPU-4GB is a budget KVM virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. It is designed for low-intensity workloads and development environments.
